intro_cards.title: は and へ as Particles
intro_cards.body:
Two characters change pronunciation when used as grammar particles:

は is normally 'ha,' but as a topic particle it's pronounced 'wa.'
へ is normally 'he,' but as a direction particle it's pronounced 'e.'

These changes only apply when the character functions as a particle. In regular words like はな (flower) or へた (unskillful), the normal pronunciation is used.

You'll see は as 'wa' in nearly every Japanese sentence.
